meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Scale Beyond Basics
content.seo_description: Break through your coding plateau. Use this intermediate SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade applications.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are building isolated features, not systems. To scale, you need a SaaS starter kit that automates authentication, database schema, and API routing. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural blueprints and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you bridge the gap between student hobbyist and professional software engineer.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List' app tutorial, but the excitement has long faded. You’ve mastered the basics, yet the leap to a production-ready SaaS feels like crossing an ocean on a raft. You are trapped in the 'tutorial purgatory,' where the code works on your machine but fails to translate into a scalable, real-world product.

It is time to stop building apps and start building systems. By adopting a professional SaaS starter kit, you shift your focus from repetitive boilerplate to high-value logic. This checklist is your blueprint for moving beyond the basics, utilizing modern AI-assisted engineering to turn your ideas into functional, market-ready software.

steps.title: Establish the Architectural Foundation
steps.description: Define your SaaS domain model. Before writing code, structure your database and auth requirements to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application including users, subscriptions, and organization tables in Prisma format.

steps.title: Implement Secure Multi-Tenant Auth
steps.description: Implement authentication that separates data by organization ID to prevent cross-tenant data leaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication flow using NextAuth that enforces organization-level data isolation and role-based access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'integrate middleware for tenant-scoped routing'

steps.title: Automate API and Route Generation
steps.description: Stop manual route creation. Use AI to scaffold your CRUD endpoints based on your domain model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold RESTful API endpoints for the 'Project' and 'Task' models, ensuring full validation with Zod.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate API routes for project management'

steps.title: Configure Stripe Subscription Lifecycle
steps.description: Handle the complex dance of webhooks and subscription states to ensure your app is monetizable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a Stripe webhook handler that updates user subscription status in the database on checkout.session.completed events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup stripe webhook listener and database sync'

steps.title: Deploy to Production with CI/CD
steps.description: Automate your testing and deployment pipeline so you can ship features faster without breaking the build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, and deploys to Vercel upon pushing to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'configure CI/CD pipeline for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always use Zod schemas for runtime validation; don't trust the client-side input ever.
Implement soft-deletes in your database so you can recover user data after accidental deletions.
Use edge functions for heavy data processing to keep your main API response times under 100ms.
Prioritize 'feature flags' to test new code in production without exposing it to all users immediately.
Keep your repository modular; separate your business logic from your UI components for easier testing.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is operational, identify one unique 'killer feature' that provides value to your target users and build it using this same modular architecture.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework, the tools, and the roadmap to build software that actually matters. Don't let this knowledge sit in a tab—apply it to your repository today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
